behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is parser validation/error-format logic at a single boundary. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none in behavior text. Rejection-gate: Integration/System were considered, but disqualified because the behavior text only requires local parsing and message construction, with no module handoff dependency, external I/O, or external side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Call parser directly with invalid cron strings and assert structured error type/message includes the specific invalid field/syntax reason.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including last-day-of-month and nearest-weekday cases.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Risk is in handoff between parsed schedule representation and calendar computation rules (especially special cron modifiers). Unit-only tests on isolated helpers can miss cross-module semantic mismatches. REAL-INFRA: no required OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System was considered but disqualified by behavior text (no external boundary or real infra requirement).
behaviors.test_description: Parse cron expressions, feed resulting schedule objects through calculator, and assert next N timestamps match expected calendars for edge modifiers across month boundaries.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags two schedules that fire within a configured minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure typically appears when schedule generation output, gap policy config, and overlap-window reporting are wired together. Unit-only checks of comparator logic may miss real handoff issues. REAL-INFRA: not required by behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because no real HTTP/subprocess/OS side effect or external readback is required.
behaviors.test_description: Construct two realistic schedules, compute upcoming run times, run overlap detection with a configured gap (e.g., 5 minutes), and assert exact conflict windows and pair attribution.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: DST correctness depends on interaction between scheduler logic and real timezone-rule handling in the time library/tz data; stubbing timezone behavior can hide errors. REAL-INFRA: no network/subprocess; this is in-process module + timezone database usage. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System considered due real-world DST complexity, but behavior text does not require external service/OS round-trip; in-process integration with actual timezone rules is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run scheduling with real timezone identifiers across known spring and fall DST transition dates; assert how nonexistent 2:30 AM is resolved in spring and how ambiguous 2:30 AM is disambiguated in fall per engine policy.

behaviors.description: If computed next run is in the past, engine applies missed-run policy to skip or backfill.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Behavior is policy orchestration across run-time computation, current-time comparison, and policy decision path (skip/backfill). Unit-only policy tests can miss state handoff defects. REAL-INFRA: no mandatory OS/network/subprocess from behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because no external boundary verification is specified; decision can be validated in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate downtime window, compute next run for jobs with different missed-run policies, and assert resulting action (skip vs backfill) and produced execution timestamps/queue entries.
